name: Implementar generación, validación y visor RIPS-FEV

description: Baseline RIPS generation benchmark with empty database (0 users, 0 encounters)

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline with empty DB. Need to seed data and re-run for meaningful measurement.

description: RIPS generation with seeded data: 10 users, 22 encounters, 1.7M COP total value. Generator queries clinical tables and builds RIPS JSON with consecutives.

asi.hypothesis: Basic RIPS generator from clinical encounters works end-to-end. Next: optimize queries, add validation tests, refine viewer.

description: Final RIPS-FEV implementation: generator + validator + viewer. 10 users, 22 encounters, 1.7M COP. All types pass, ultracite clean, tests green.

asi.hypothesis: Complete implementation of RIPS generation, preflight validation, and structured viewer in frontend. Ready for MUV integration and XML/JSON submission.

description: Relational query rewrite: single query with joins instead of 5 separate queries. Result: 12ms (worse than 9ms baseline) due to SQLite join overhead at small scale (22 encounters).

asi.hypothesis: Using Drizzle relational queries to reduce DB roundtrips from 5 to 1
asi.rollback_reason: SQLite join overhead at small scale outweighs benefit; 12ms vs 9ms previous best. Relational queries are a scalability win for large datasets but not for current benchmark scale.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on JS-level optimizations: precompute diagnosis lookups once per encounter instead of 5x per builder. Also consider caching SISPRO catalog lookups in validator for overall RIPS workflow speed.
